petitioner_background: The petitioner sought classification as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act as an alien of extraordinary ability in the sciences. No further details about the petitioner's specific field, nationality, or career qualifications are provided in the decision. The petition was filed with the Texas Service Center and denied by the Director.
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was summarily dismissed because counsel failed to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact. On appeal, counsel merely stated 'See Brief that will be submitted in 30 days,' but despite the appeal being filed on July 6, 2009, more than sixteen months had passed with no further submission received by the AAO. Counsel did not specifically challenge any of the director's findings or analyses of the evidence submitted for the regulatory criteria at 8 C.F.R. § 204.5(h)(3), and the appellate submission was unaccompanied by arguments or evidence addressing those criteria. Pursuant to 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v), an appeal shall be summarily dismissed if the party concerned fails to identify specifically any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact.
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural dismissal: Counsel filed the appeal on July 6, 2009 stating only 'See Brief that will be submitted in 30 days,' but failed to submit any brief or supporting evidence in the more than sixteen months that followed. Counsel did not specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact, warranting summary dismissal under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v).
